metadata.title: Agent Skill OS
metadata.description: Install battle-tested skills into your AI coding agent in 30 seconds.

footer.label: Footer navigation
footer.tagline: Reusable expert skills for AI coding agents.

home.eyebrow: Open-source skill registry for AI coding agents
home.tagline: Install battle-tested skills into your AI coding agent in 30 seconds.
home.worksWithBody: Generic folders, Claude, Codex, and Cursor target adapters.
home.whyBody: Prompt snippets become installable, versioned, reusable skills.
home.localBody: No account, database, marketplace, or model call is required for the MVP.

skillsPage.title: Browse, search, and install reusable AI agent skills.
skillsPage.description: Every skill is Markdown with typed metadata, target compatibility, and a repeatable workflow.

skillDetail.originalLanguageNotice: Skill content is currently shown in its original language.

packsPage.title: Install a complete workflow in one command.
packsPage.description: Packs combine related skills for developer productivity, repo maintenance, launches, and video creation.

developer-productivity.name: Developer Productivity
developer-productivity.summary: Essential skills for daily AI-assisted development.

repo-maintainer.name: Repo Maintainer
repo-maintainer.summary: Skills for keeping issues, pull requests, releases, and docs moving.

launch-kit.name: Launch Kit
launch-kit.summary: Skills for turning a project idea into launch-ready assets.

ai-video-creator.name: AI Video Creator
ai-video-creator.summary: Skills for planning technical demos, scripts, titles, and source summaries.

install.title: Install together
install.body: A pack installs related skills with one command.

route.title: Route selectively
route.body: The runtime router chooses the relevant skill for each task.

load.title: Load few
load.body: Agents should load only the selected skill and at most two supporting skills.

targetsPage.title: Install skills into the agent you already use.
targetsPage.description: Each target adapter writes skill files, runtime metadata, and loader instructions in the format your coding agent expects.

codex.name: Codex
codex.summary: Install skills into `.codex/skills` and update `AGENTS.md` with runtime routing instructions.

claude.name: Claude
claude.summary: Install skills into `.claude/skills` and generate Claude-facing runtime usage guidance.

cursor.name: Cursor
cursor.summary: Install skills as Cursor rules with a project-level Agent Skill OS loader.

generic.name: Generic
generic.summary: Install portable Markdown skills into `agent-skills` for any agent or workflow.

install.title: Install
install.body: The CLI writes the skill, manifest, lockfile, router, and target loader into the project.

route.title: Route
route.body: The agent reads `.agent-skill-os/router.json` and selects one primary skill for the task.

load.title: Load
load.body: The agent loads only the selected skill's `SKILL.md`, keeping context focused.

useCasesPage.title: Discover installable workflows by outcome.
useCasesPage.description: Use cases group skills into practical workflows for code review, repo maintenance, launches, and technical content.

code-review.name: Code Review
code-review.summary: Install focused review, test gap, bug reproduction, and PR summary skills for pull request workflows.

developer-productivity.name: Developer Productivity
developer-productivity.summary: Give a coding agent code review, testing, bug reproduction, refactoring, README, and PR summary skills.

open-source-launch.name: Open-source Launch
open-source-launch.summary: Turn a project into launch-ready README, PRD, landing copy, launch posts, and demo plans.

repo-maintenance.name: Repo Maintenance
repo-maintenance.summary: Keep issues, pull requests, releases, changelogs, and contribution docs moving.

ai-video-creator.name: AI Video Creator
ai-video-creator.summary: Plan technical demos, short scripts, titles, thumbnails, and source summaries.

install.title: Install once
install.body: A pack installs related skills together while the runtime still loads only the skill needed for each task.

routing.title: Runtime routing
routing.body: The generated router keeps the agent from loading every installed skill by default.

outputs.code-review: Structured review findings, risk levels, missing test recommendations, reproduction notes, and PR summaries.
outputs.developer-productivity: Review findings, regression tests, reproduction steps, refactor plans, README drafts, and PR summaries.
outputs.open-source-launch: PRD drafts, README improvements, landing copy, launch posts, and demo video plans.
outputs.repo-maintenance: Issue triage, PR summaries, release notes, changelog drafts, and contribution guide updates.
outputs.ai-video-creator: Short video scripts, demo outlines, YouTube title ideas, thumbnail copy, and source summaries.

marketplacePage.title: Browse skills with source, quality, license, and reporting signals.
marketplacePage.description: This is not a paid marketplace. It is the trust foundation for discovering reusable AI agent skills safely.

official.title: Source level
official.body: Built-in skills are marked official; external registries can be labeled later.

quality.title: Static quality
quality.body: Grades reuse the deterministic `aso quality` checks.

report.title: Safety reports
report.body: Unsafe or misleading skills can be reported through a dedicated issue template.

showcase.title: Three installable workflows for the first 30 seconds.
showcase.description: Each scenario shows the command, installed skills, expected output, and why it matters for an AI coding agent.

developerProductivity.name: Developer Productivity
developerProductivity.output: Review findings, regression tests, repro steps, refactor plans, README drafts, and PR summaries.
developerProductivity.useful: Gives a coding agent a complete daily development workflow without copy-pasting old prompts.

launchKit.name: Launch Kit
launchKit.output: PRDs, README polish, landing copy, launch posts, and demo video plans.
launchKit.useful: Turns a rough project idea into launch-ready GitHub and social assets.

aiVideoCreator.name: AI Video Creator
aiVideoCreator.output: Short scripts, demo outlines, YouTube titles, thumbnail text, and source summaries.
aiVideoCreator.useful: Helps creators package technical projects into watchable demos and reusable content.

docsPage.title: Install skills, create new ones, and validate your registry.
docsPage.skillFormatBody: Skills live at skills/<skill-id>/SKILL.md with frontmatter metadata and required Markdown sections.
docsPage.targetAdaptersBody: Generic writes to agent-skills/, Claude writes to .claude/skills/, Codex writes to .codex/skills/, and Cursor writes .mdc rules.
docsPage.contributingBody: Run pnpm validate and pnpm test before opening a pull request. Use docs/contributing-skill.md for the full skill contribution guide.
docsPage.roadmapBody: v1.0 establishes the stable schema, CLI, runtime files, target adapters, MCP server, quality checks, migration guide, and security baseline.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.summary: How agents route, select, and load installed skills without reading everything.
runtime.concept: The runtime is the local layer made of router.json, skill-index.json, usage.md, and target loaders. Its policy is install many, load few.
runtime.contract: Agents select one primary skill, at most two supporting skills, state the selection, load only relevant SKILL.md files, and validate against the Runtime Contract.

registry.title: Registry
registry.summary: How local and remote registries describe skills and packs.
registry.concept: A registry is typed metadata plus skill and pack entries. Remote registries are JSON indexes that point to raw SKILL.md files.
registry.contract: Remote skills are text instructions, not executable code. The CLI shows source URLs and writes source information to the lockfile.

mcp.title: MCP Server
mcp.summary: How agents can discover, recommend, load, install, and validate skills through MCP.
mcp.concept: The MCP server exposes Agent Skill OS runtime operations over stdio JSON-RPC.
mcp.contract: MCP tools follow the same runtime policy as the CLI: recommend one primary skill and avoid loading every installed skill by default.
